entry.title: Re: how can I use a cursor on an initially empty, growing...
entry.content:
I'm not sure if this is a good way to do it (I'm a newbee to SQL, especially HANA), but this is how got this to work:

CREATE OR REPLACE PROCEDURE "q_FollowLinkedTables"
/**
 * List BaseEntries and TargetEntries of a given B1 Entry/Type combination
 * V2.2 Coded 2026-05-20 by .rhavin;)
 */
(
  IN  EntryNumber int,
  IN  EntryType   int,
  IN  Backwards   BOOLEAN,
  OUT Result      "q_tt_EntryMap"
)
/* type q_tt_EntryMap is a table (Entry,Type) */
LANGUAGE SQLSCRIPT 
SQL SECURITY INVOKER

AS
  LineTable     NVARCHAR(10);
  tSQL          NVARCHAR(4000);
  ActualTab     "q_tt_EntryMap";
  AllTab        "q_tt_EntryMap";
  TypeField     NVARCHAR(10);
  NumberField   NVARCHAR(10);
  counter       int;
  CURSOR curPos FOR SELECT * FROM :ActualTab;
BEGIN

  counter := 0;
  IF :Backwards = TRUE THEN
    TypeField    := 'BaseType';
    NumberField  := 'BaseEntry';
  ELSE
    TypeField    := 'TargetType';
    NumberField  := 'TrgetEntry';
  END IF;
  /* This fn returns "INV" for 13 and so on */
  LineTable    := "q_GetObjTable"(EntryType) || '1';
  
  WHILE :LineTable IS NOT NULL
  DO
    tSQL := '
    SELECT DISTINCT "'|| :NumberField ||'" AS "Entry", "'|| :TypeField ||'" AS "Type"
    FROM ' || :LineTable || '
    WHERE "DocEntry" = ' || :EntryNumber || ' AND "'|| :TypeField ||'" <> -1
    ';
    EXECUTE IMMEDIATE tSQL INTO ActualTab;
    /* here I push the (perhaps multiple) results into AllTab */
    FOR entry AS curPos
    DO
      INSERT INTO :AllTab VALUES (entry."Entry", entry."Type");
    END FOR;

    /* here I get the next value at counter's pos from  */
    SELECT "Entry" ,"Type"
    INTO EntryNumber, EntryType DEFAULT NULL,NULL
    FROM :AllTab
    LIMIT 1 OFFSET :counter;

    counter := counter + 1;
    
    LineTable := "q_GetObjTable"(EntryType) || '1';
    
    /* reasonable safeguard to prevent recursive locks */
    IF :counter > 15 THEN
      BREAK;
    END IF;

  END WHILE;

  Result = SELECT * FROM :AllTab;

END;

entry.title: Re: yaai - ABAP AI tools - Evolving Toward Agentic AI
entry.content:
Hi @ChristianSilva ,

 

its all about exposure of SAP functionality to external applications (MCP clients)

In that case you are not interacting with LLM directly on ABAP stack - during which you can refer to tools / functions - which can be used by LLM so you are using its response to call up e.g. class methods dynamically.

For instance, you have a custom development implemented in ABAP and you are not (only) expose an API (OData / REST) with which you can work with your business object from an external system, but you are exposing your agents configured in yaai so your custom development / business object acts as MCP Server.

This is just an idea, maybe I overlook or mix up things 🙂

entry.title: Launch Transactions from Web UI if the Browser Version is NOT Compatible with the SAP Version
entry.content:
Background:

The GUI Transaction getting launched from Web UI via Transaction Launcher might throw an error "500 Internal Server Error" and not get launched properly.

On analysis, it was found that this was due to Non-Compatibility between the SAP Version of the Target system and the Internet Browser Version.

In this blog, I have explained how to resolve the error and launch the SAP GUI Transactions in case you get error due to incompatibility with browser version.

Analysis of the Error: 

  •  On analysis, it was found that this was due to Non-Compatibility between the SAP Version of the Target system and the Internet Browser Version.
  • To find the reason for the error, I went to F12->Developer tools -> Emulation Tab and checked the Browser Version. It was IE5. On selecting the Browser Version to IE9 from the dropdown the GUI Transaction in the Target system got launched properly.
  • Based on this it was Concluded that Non-Compatibility between SAP Target system Version and Browser version is the reason for this Error.
    • Suggested Solutions:
    • If we resolve by upgrading the browser version to IE9 it would involve lot of Impact analysis across all SAP and Non-SAP system and the solution is very time consuming.
    • To resolve this error, we can either upgrade the browser Version to IE9 (or) launch the ECC Transaction in the Target system using JavaScript. Open approach.
    • If we resolve by upgrading the browser version to IE9 it would involve lot of Impact analysis across all SAP and Non-SAP system and the solution to be ready for End User was very time consuming.
    • Hence the second approach to launch the Transaction using javascript.open approach was followed.
    • Steps for the Solution:
    • In TCode se11 build a Custom Database table to hold the Parameter name, System name and the date from which the New Logic must be active. Build a Table Maintenance generator for this table.
    • Then go to TCode BSP_WD_CMPWB and check if the Component CRMCMP_NAVBAR is enhanced. If it is not enhanced, then enhance the component.
    • Enhance the View ShortCutLinks.
    • Go to the.htm page and check if the current date sy-datum is greater than the date from which the New Logic must be triggered.
    • If yes, then the New Logic will be triggered. If not, the New Logic will not be triggered.
    • Call the method get_shortcuttable and get the values of the NavBar Profile, Work Center, Work Center group links, Logic links into an Internal table lt_dlgs (from Internal table ct_dlgs ).
    • Create a Custom class in se24 having four methods to generate the URL at runtime.
    • The Four Custom methods are:
      • ModifyShortcut
      • GetURL_BY_IDS
      • BuildURLS
      • BuildJS4URLS

      •  Method ModifyShortcut:  

      • In this method, I first read the value of the logical link and pass the relevant values to the next method GET_URL_BY_ID.

      • Method GetURL_BY_IDS:

      • In this method, I got the Protocol, URL, Logical system by selecting from the database table CRMC_IC_LTX_URL and CRMC_IC_LTX_URLT. If the Protocol is http, I assigned http to a variable. If the protocol is https, I assigned https to a variable.

      • Method BuildURLS:

      • In this method I got the RFC destination of the remote system by calling the method GET_RFCDEST_FROM_LOGSYS of the class CL_CRM_IC_LTX_REMOTECONBASE. Then I used the method create_url of the class cl_crm_web_utility and passed the Protocol, RFC Destination, generated the URL and assigned to a Variable rv_result.

      •  Then I assigned ‘WINDOW’ to another Variable lv_window.

      •  Then I called the method BUILDJS4URL by passing the variable rv_result and lv_window.

      • Method BuildJS4URLS:

      • In this method I assigned the string give below to a variable lv_link.

        lv_link  = Javascript:window.Open (&&rv_result&&, lv_window , width, height, resizable =1, scrollbars = 1).

      •  This variable lv_link generated in the method BuildJs4URL was assigned to the parameter ONCLIENTCLICK in the Internal table lt_dlgs of the method Modifyshortcuts .

      • By this when the User tries to launch the ECC transaction the Component CRMCMP_NAVBAR and View Getshortlinks get triggered. In the .htm page the validation of the current date is greater than (or) equal to the date in the Custom table is done and if yes, then the method get_shortcuttable is called and the custom class methods are called and the url is generated and assigned to the Parameter ONCLIENTCLICK.

        By following the above approach, I was able to launch GUI Transaction without having to change the browser version.

                                            *End of Document*
